summary: Threat-model walk over the per-actor channel architecture. One Medium (Channel.Stream.ReadAllBytesAsync ignores Channel.Buffer; unbounded read), one Low (MigrationEnvelope.Signature is keyless SHA256 with PKI-shaped fields and a doc/impl mismatch — VerifyEnvelope returns true for any forged envelope), and three Notes (Variables.Snapshot use site under standing Sensitive-leak finding; MigrationEnvelope.Payload polymorphic-deserialize concern for future receive side; encoding fallback silent). Zero High, zero Critical. F1 turns into a real OOM vector the moment Stream channels wrap untrusted network bodies; F2 becomes a complete trust bypass the moment FromMigration ships if VerifyEnvelope is treated as a gate.

prior_findings_re_verified.codeanalyzer_v3_B1_static_to_instance: closed (probe test added in coder v8)
prior_findings_re_verified.codeanalyzer_v3_L1_copy_on_write_Enter: closed (probe test added in coder v8)

next_bot_reason: Independent re-verification of F1 (Stream Buffer cap) and F2 (MigrationEnvelope misleading-signature) before any code change lands — same security→auditor→coder pattern used on runtime2-callback. Skip auditor and go straight to coder if the F2 cleanup (delete VerifyEnvelope, rename inner field to IntegrityHash, drop identity fields) feels uncontroversial.
